ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛtsã Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a abuĩ. Ede ɛnaa katɔbii kadi akũ ade ɛkɛna akũ edzi a.
translated: Jesus came to his own town in Galilee. Then, hearing that he had come, he went to him.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Ɛmɔ́ kĩ kɛkapiã ye bati a mɔ ɔkɔlɔ a kahali, kitonɛkĩ ɔwɛ kavũ katsɛlamɔ be. Foesũ fɔmɔ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ elo aabɔa a, Yesu tsã mui anɛ ɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃. Kĩ efuã be a, ɛkɛna ndɛ yatɛ̃ be,
translated: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field. But while people were sleeping, an enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat. When the plants came up and bore grain, the owner said to the servants...

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Boe Ɔlate, yĩ ɔsumunɛ waa nɛ kɔla yasɔ, yaápuli ekuna akũ, ade ɛlamɔ lubo kamɔ paa a.”
translated: "Greetings Olate, your message has reached us, we have gathered here, and we are speaking well of your good deeds."

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Kĩ bɛkanya a, Yesu bɔɛ abodoo, ade kĩ ɛbɔa mpaɛ nɛ ye akũ a, ebulibuli ye ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a, ebi be kĩ, “Mitɛ minya, ade yĩ sukɛna nɛ awĩ a.”
translated: And behold, Jesus came to teach, and when he saw the multitude, he said to them, "My children, do not be afraid among you."

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ a, Seba Lɛgãtsole aayidza ɛdza ɛkɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɛtsã osi gɛlɛɛlɛ eto ye ɔmaĩ kamɛ ɛyatse Lɛgã Salomo nyansã fɔtsa katsa, ade mitɛ yĩ mido kĩ odi deĩ nɛ awĩ kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Salomo. Hũhũ Kpa Kabuki Kamuniki Kaya.
translated: In Kadzɛ Luwi, where Seba Lɛgãtsole was born, there came a time when a great leader ruled with wisdom, as King Solomon did in his time, known for his just reign. So it was said that even today, he is indeed Solomon. Thus concludes the tale of Kpa Kabuki Kamuniki Kaya.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: ade ebi ye kĩ, “Yidza, kakpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma mimuniki minaa Israelfɔ tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ bikĩ bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ obisɛ̃ a ku.”
translated: Yidza, if you follow my commandments and keep the laws of the Israelites until the end, then they will know that you have obeyed me.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Midã kɔvɔlabi, kɔádu funitsã, kɔákpɔta funitsã kako nɛ tili kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a adɔɔla koe! Mbɔɛɛ mitátɛ kɔya miba kɔvɔlabi?
translated: Midã arrives, leading the people, gathering the people, but the path is blocked. I beg Oléga to show the way for me! Where is the truth that you will come again?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Fɔmɔ aana kutse libi wĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ana kutse wĩ, ade ntɛ odeĩ kutse kpa ni, kutse libi kpa aana. Babi a kĩ kutse awũ a bakɔa kanyi kutse a odu.
translated: "They say that the path exists, but it does not exist; the truth is that it has vanished, so the path is gone. Babi says that the thing is kept hidden as a secret."

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Bati pii aamuniki nɛ be katɛkado liti. Baanyɛ be akũ akũ abɔa, bikesĩ akũ lɛmɛ.
translated: The pure water from the source flows through the valley. The trees are deeply rooted and stand firm, their branches spreading widely.

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Lɛmɛ Petro sĩ nɛ bati a flee anɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ĩnyina lɛtsama nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ alɛ a akũ.”
translated: Lemë Petro said, "Even though the path is difficult, this is the way we must go."

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Kĩ sodzafɔ a bɔa ye bɛmɛna nɛ asendua akũ biyua a, bɛta bavulibi nɛ ye tɛtadiɛ akũ, bɛsɛ̃ bɛkpa akũ.
translated: When the journey comes to an end and the way is clear, but the people have not yet reached their destination, they continue to move forward.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ke liti a, ɛnyɛ be osi kĩ bɛnaa Betlehem mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, “Minaawɛ obisɛ̃ a kawɛ wĩ. Ntɛ mimɔ́ ye ni, mifɔ̃ ĩnyi lɔkɔa yĩ malo nnaasum ye.”
translated: In those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered. So everyone went to their own town to be enrolled. Joseph also went up from Nazareth in Galilee to Bethlehem in Judea, because he was of the house and lineage of David.

ref: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
text: Kito lɔkɔ a naa, ɛkawɛ osi wĩ a kĩ yaatsã ɛnyɛ Yesu ɛkpa. Yesu Mɔ Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Nya Wuda Katɛ̃ Funitsã a.
translated: Kito comes to us, and we see the salvation of Jesus the Christ. Jesus, the Lord of Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ, the Cross of Katɛ̃ Funitsã.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ ade awã aana flee kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ose kɔtɔ a flee oyua a. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Kɔtsɔ̃ Mɔ Kɔnyɔ Liti Kanɛ Akũ.
translated: Jesus the Christ, the Covenant of God, who brings light to all of us.

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Kĩ Yesu nu foe a, ɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “O, boe ɔma, nɔ katɛkado pɔnɛ! Lɛtsa a kĩ awɛ a lɛya lɛkpa nɔ.” Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ a obitsole a akũ de ɔsĩ. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee.
translated: And Jesus said to them, “O my mother, why have you forsaken me! This is the one who has betrayed me.” Then, as they wept bitterly, they fulfilled what was spoken of him. Jesus the Son of Beblebee.
